completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/adapters/guardkit/models.py declaring both classes as Pydantic v2 BaseModels. TestModelsDefinedInModelsModule asserts `GuardKitResult.__module__ == 'forge.adapters.guardkit.models'`, the same for GuardKitWarning, and that `inspect.getsourcefile()` returns a path ending in `forge/adapters/guardkit/models.py`.

GuardKitResult.status is annotated `Literal['success','failed','timeout']`. TestStatusFieldIsLiteralNotEnum verifies via `typing.get_origin` that the origin is `Literal` and the args are exactly `{'success','failed','timeout'}`, and that an unknown string ('errored') raises ValidationError.

Both list fields declared as `Field(default_factory=list)`. TestListFieldsUseDefaultFactory parametrises across both fields, asserting `model_fields[name].default_factory is list`, and proves mutation isolation by appending to one instance and checking another instance is still empty. The supporting `GuardKitWarning.details` dict field is similarly verified.

All four fields declared with `| None = None`. TestOptionalFieldsAreNullable parametrises across all four, asserting they default to None on a minimal instance, accept explicit None, and that their resolved type hints contain `type(None)` in the union args.

TestRoundTripsThroughJSON exercises the round-trip on (a) a minimal instance, (b) a fully-populated instance with non-empty artefacts, criterion_breakdown, detection_findings, stderr, stdout_tail and a nested GuardKitWarning carrying a non-empty details dict, and (c) each Literal status value. Each test asserts equality between the original and the rebuilt model.

Created src/forge/adapters/guardkit/__init__.py importing `GuardKitResult` and `GuardKitWarning` from `.models` and listing them in __all__, mirroring the `forge.config.__init__` shim called out in Implementation Notes. TestPackageReExportShim asserts `forge.adapters.guardkit.GuardKitResult is models.GuardKitResult` (and same for GuardKitWarning) and that both names appear in `__all__`.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: All new files compile cleanly under `python3 -m py_compile` (exit 0). All lines fit within the project's 88-character Black line limit (verified by awk scan: zero offenders across models.py, __init__.py, and test_models.py). Module docstrings, snake_case naming, and type hints follow .claude/rules/code-style.md. Black itself is not installed in any of the discovered environments on this host (.guardkit/venv has no pip-installed dev deps and there is no top-level .venv), so format equivalence was verified mechanically rather than via the Black binary.
